word_list.word: better one house spoiled than two
word_list.phonetic_text: b蓻.t蓽r n ha蕣s sp蓴瑟ld 冒忙n tu:
word_list.phonetic_am_text: be.t膿r n haoos spawild dh膩n too

senses.definition: used to imply that it is better to sacrifice one thing to prevent the destruction or damage of two things, emphasizing the importance of making tough choices and prioritizing what is most valuable

examples.x: I had to end my partnership with my friend, but it was better one house spoiled than two - if we had continued to work together, it would have ruined our friendship and our business.
